title: Dhūmāvatī Sādhana
subcategory: Mahāvidyā (seventh) — Smoke-clad widow / goddess of inauspiciousness
century: Medieval (c. 9th–14th c.); digest literature c. 16th–19th c.
description: The seventh of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Dhūmāvatī ("she who is composed of smoke") is iconographically an aged, smoke-complexioned widow riding a horseless chariot bearing a crow banner, holding a winnowing basket. High caution — an antinomian Mahāvidyā rite whose iconography deliberately encodes inauspiciousness (widowhood, hunger, decay, smoke, the crow) as a vehicle of liberating insight. Preserved
